source.multipart_basis: eISR wet-hull submarines are built as bonded GFRP shell assemblies with discrete domes at bow/stern, internal ring frames for buckling stiffness, a bolted pilot hatch ring, viewport housings, bolted thruster / control-fin pod mounts, and molded-in rudder/fin brackets. The single-body brief treats the shell as one GFRP monocoque; this multipart decomposition splits it into the bow dome, aft dome, cylindrical pressure-hull section (wet-hull), internal frames, hatch ring, viewport housings, thruster / fin pod mounts, and fin/rudder brackets. Speculative at the level of internal frame count; load magnitudes, material, and pass/fail thresholds are preserved.

full_prompt:
Design the main hull (monocoque shell) for an eISR (European International Submarine Races) human-powered submarine, compliant with eISR24 Rulebook. The pilot operates on SCUBA inside a wet submarine (flooded hull). Geometric constraints per eISR24: overall hull length 3.0 m, maximum beam (diameter) 0.85 m (length-to-beam ratio 3.53:1, within rule limits), height 0.85 m. Hull is a one-piece GFRP (glass fiber reinforced plastic) hand-laid shell, [0/90/+45/-45]s quasi-isotropic layup with 0.5 mm plies, 12 plies total (6 mm shell thickness). Material: E-glass/polyester; E1 = 25 GPa, E2 = 8 GPa, long-tensile strength 400 MPa, long-compressive strength 300 MPa, density 1900 kg/m^3. Operating depth 5 m (pool); max planned depth 10 m for structural margin. Load cases: (R1) Hydrostatic at 10 m (100 kPa external differential, interior flooded, so net differential = 0 across the shell for a wet submarine -- the rule here is actually inertial + steering loads, not pressure); (R2) Pilot pedal effort: 500 N forward through the bulkhead propulsion mount; max fiber stress <= 400 / 3.0 = 133 MPa. (R3) Fin bending: aft control fins (deflectable by pilot) apply 200 N at 200 mm from hull centerline = 40 N*m moment at the fin-mount; max fiber stress at mount <= 133 MPa. (R4) Ballast release: mechanical dead-man releases a 5 kg ballast weight (5 x 9.81 = 49 N) from the hull; the mounting lug must survive 2x dynamic amplification = 98 N without permanent damage; max fiber stress <= 133 MPa. (R5) Linear buckling eigenvalue under external-pressure equivalent of 100 kPa (the wet-hull case is slightly more complex but model as crush check) >= 3.0. (R6) Positive buoyancy reserve: vehicle without pilot must float positively (rule requires at least +5 kgf buoyancy empty); displaced volume - structural mass - ballast <= -5 kg equivalent; geometric check. (R7) Hull structural mass <= 35 kg. (R8) SCUBA integration: clear internal volume behind the pilot's bulkhead >= 200 L for tanks and regulators; geometric check. Primary verification: laminate composite structural FEA for R2-R4 + linear buckling for R5. Vibration, thermal, fluid, radiation NOT required. Deliverables: STEP AP242 hull, laminate schedule, and a report listing hull mass in kg, max fiber stress per case in MPa, first buckling load factor, buoyancy reserve in kgf, internal volume in L.

--- ASSEMBLY RECONFIGURATION ---
eISR wet-hull submarines are bonded GFRP shell assemblies, not single-piece hand-laid shells, once the hatch, viewport, and fin pickups are included. Speculated families: (A) forward dome (bow hemispherical cap closing the front of the hull, molded GFRP with local ply build-up); (B) aft dome (stern hemispherical cap carrying the control-fin brackets and aft thruster / propeller); (C) cylindrical pressure-hull section (central GFRP monocoque tube, ~2 m long, 0.85 m diameter, wet-hull so pressure-equalized); (D) internal ring frames (x4, bonded circumferentially inside C, stiffen against LC4 crush buckling); (E) hatch ring (bolted ring on top of C receiving the bolted pilot hatch for crew access); (F) viewport housings (x2, forward port/starboard, GFRP housings bonded/bolted to C with O-ring viewport seat); (G) thruster / fin pod mounts (aft, bolted to B + C interface, carrying LC3 fin bending and propulsion thrust reaction); (H) fin / rudder brackets (x4 aft control surfaces, bolted to B aft dome); (J) propulsion-mount bulkhead (bonded internal bulkhead receiving the LC1 pilot pedal effort); (K) ballast lug (metal insert bonded into B lower surface, carries LC3 dynamic ballast release). Interfaces: A<->C bow dome bonded; B<->C aft dome bonded; D<->C ring frames fillet bonded; E<->C hatch ring bolted through bonded-in studs; F<->C viewport housings bolted with O-ring; G<->B/C thruster mount bolted; H<->B fin brackets bolted; J<->C propulsion bulkhead bonded internally; K<->B ballast lug bonded insert. FEM verification runs on the assembled model ONLY per fem_check_policy=assembled_only. Under LC2 pedal effort enters J, flows into C; LC3 fin bending into H brackets stresses B aft dome and aft D ring frame; LC4 ballast release loads K which is bonded to B; LC5 crush buckling eigenvalue depends on D ring-frame spacing on C. Load magnitudes (500 N LC1, 40 Nm LC2, 98 N LC3, 100 kPa LC4), material (E-glass/polyester, 400 MPa long-tensile), and stress/buckling/mass/buoyancy/volume thresholds are preserved verbatim.
short_prompt:
Design the main hull (monocoque shell) for an eISR (European International Submarine Races) human-powered submarine as a multi-part bonded GFRP assembly, compliant with eISR24 Rulebook. Pilot operates on SCUBA inside a wet (flooded) submarine.

Overall hull length 3.0 m, maximum beam 0.85 m (L:B 3.53:1), height 0.85 m. GFRP shell quasi-isotropic [0/90/+45/-45]s, 0.5 mm plies, 12 plies total (6 mm shell thickness). Material E-glass/polyester: E1 = 25 GPa, E2 = 8 GPa, long-tensile 400 MPa, long-compressive 300 MPa, density 1900 kg/m^3. Operating depth 5 m (pool); structural margin depth 10 m.

LC1 pilot pedal effort: 500 N forward through the propulsion bulkhead; max fiber stress <= 400 / 3.0 = 133 MPa. LC2 fin bending: aft control fins 200 N at 200 mm from centerline = 40 N*m; <= 133 MPa. LC3 ballast release: 5 kg mechanical deadman (49 N) with 2x dynamic = 98 N; <= 133 MPa. LC4 crush buckling eigenvalue under 100 kPa external equivalent >= 3.0. Positive buoyancy reserve empty >= 5 kgf. Hull structural mass <= 35 kg. Clear internal volume behind pilot bulkhead >= 200 L for SCUBA tanks/regulators.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: R6 buoyancy reserve and R8 internal clear volume are geometric CAD volume calculations.

prompt.title: eISR24 human-powered submarine hull (multi-part bonded assembly)
prompt.brief: GFRP wet-hull submarine assembly, 3.0 m x 0.85 m beam; pilot-powered with pedal effort, aft control fins, ballast release, and crush buckling check. Multi-part: forward dome (A), aft dome (B), cylindrical pressure hull (C), internal ring frames (D x4), hatch ring (E), viewport housings (F x2), thruster/fin pod mount (G), fin/rudder brackets (H x4), propulsion bulkhead (J), ballast lug (K). FEM assembled-only; DCR <= 1.0.

material.name: E-glass / polyester GFRP
material.adhesive: marine-grade structural epoxy; shear allowable ~18 MPa
material.viewport_material: cast acrylic 20 mm with EPDM O-ring seat
material.metal_insert_material: 316 stainless at K ballast lug and E hatch studs

boundary_conditions.mounting: internal pilot bulkhead J fixed for structural loads LC1; for LC2/LC3 the hull is supported at hatch ring E to avoid rigid-body motion; LC4 is free with external uniform pressure
boundary_conditions.loads: concentrated at propulsion, fin, ballast points; uniform pressure for LC4
boundary_conditions.bonded_joints: A<->C, B<->C, D<->C, J<->C, K<->B as tied contact with adhesive shear post-check

output_format.body_count: multi-body (A + B + C + D x4 + E + F x2 + G + H x4 + J + K)

parts.name: forward_dome
parts.role: bow hemispherical cap closing the front of the hull

geometric_constraints.construction: GFRP hand lay-up with local ply build-up at the bond flange

parts.name: aft_dome
parts.role: stern hemispherical cap carrying the control-fin brackets, thruster pod mount, and ballast lug

geometric_constraints.construction: GFRP with bonded-in stainless ballast lug K and bolted H fin brackets

parts.name: cylindrical_pressure_hull
parts.role: central cylindrical GFRP tube between A and B; main pilot compartment

parts.name: internal_ring_frame
parts.role: circumferential ring bonded inside C to stiffen against LC4 crush buckling

geometric_constraints.bond: fillet bond lay-up to C inner surface

parts.material_hint: GFRP rigid foam core + glass overwrap

parts.name: hatch_ring
parts.role: bolted ring on top of C receiving the pilot hatch; service access and BC for LC2/LC3

parts.name: viewport_housing
parts.role: forward port/starboard viewport housings for pilot visibility

geometric_constraints.mount: bolted through C with 8 M5 bolts per viewport

parts.name: thruster_fin_pod_mount
parts.role: aft pod carrying propeller and aft control fin mounts; bolted at B-C interface
parts.material_hint: GFRP pod with Al 6061-T6 insert plate

parts.name: fin_rudder_bracket
parts.role: aft control-surface bracket bolted to B aft dome; carries LC2 fin bending

parts.name: propulsion_mount_bulkhead
parts.role: internal bulkhead bonded inside C where pilot pedals feed thrust into the hull

geometric_constraints.bond: fillet bonded circumferentially to C inner surface

parts.name: ballast_lug
parts.role: bonded stainless lug on B lower surface; releasable ballast deadman attach; carries LC3

assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X forward (surge), Y port-starboard (sway), Z up (heave)

pass_fail_criteria.description: Connection DCR across bonded/bolted interfaces (IF1..IF9) <= 1.0 under the governing load case.
Dome-to-cylinder bonds (IF1, IF2) adhesive shear utilization <= 1.0 under LC4 crush and LC2 fin-bending load distribution.
pass_fail_criteria.metric: max adhesive shear utilization at IF1, IF2

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Generated coverage gate: ensures prompt-level envelope, body-count, report-field, and part geometry constraints are represented by an explicit geometry audit experiment.

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: laminated shell for A/B/C/D/F/J + solid for K, H, E metal parts
connection_modeling: dome-to-cylinder bonds as tied contact with adhesive shear post-check
ring frames D bonded to C inner skin as tied contact; influences LC4 buckling eigenvalue directly
hatch ring E and viewport F bolts as stiffness-equivalent MPC/CBUSH
bonded metal inserts K as tied solid-to-shell

interfaces.mediated_by: continuous structural adhesive bond with overlap lay-up
interfaces.load_path: forward closure; reacts LC4 axial crush component at the bow

interfaces.mediated_by: continuous structural adhesive bond with overlap lay-up
interfaces.load_path: aft closure; transfers H fin bracket loads and G thruster pod loads into C
stiffens C against LC4 crush buckling; governs R5

interfaces.mediated_by: bolted through bonded-in stainless studs; gasketed
interfaces.load_path: hatch service access; bolted BC for LC2/LC3 analysis
pilot viewport; minor structural share, stress-concentrator check in LC4
propulsion thrust reaction into aft hull
LC2 fin-bending moment (40 Nm) from control surface into B aft dome
LC1 500 N pilot pedal effort from J into C through the bond

interfaces.mediated_by: bonded stainless insert with film adhesive
interfaces.load_path: LC3 98 N dynamic ballast release into B

fem_check_policy.rationale: Wet-hull submarines have their real stress state at dome-to-cylinder bonds, hatch ring bolts, viewport cutouts, and bonded inserts. LC4 crush buckling is particularly sensitive to internal ring-frame spacing D on C. A single-shell FEA would miss all of these.

notes.why_multi_part: Real eISR wet submarines are bonded GFRP assemblies with discrete hatch rings, viewports, and aft fin pickups; hand-laid single-shell idealization overstates buckling margin because ring frames are not represented.
notes.speculation_caveats: Ring-frame count (4) and hatch diameter (600 mm) are plausible defaults; actual geometry is team-specific. No additional eISR rulebook clauses are cited.

generated_eval_coverage.purpose: Add runnable CCX coverage kit for JSON-only multipart prompt.
generated_eval_coverage.note: Generated checks assert solver coverage for every FEA-grade requirement, every declared load case, and prompt-level geometry constraints; they are not a domain-specific certification model.

fea_experiments.metric: max adhesive shear utilization at IF1, IF2
